posts.text: Not saying IEA has been consistantly wrong in their demand forecasts for the last decade. But, 2025 "actual demand" has already surpassed their 2030 demand forecast. (2020 should be negated all together, no one could have forcasted that) https://t.co/2YmyGCQdNx
posts.summary: The International Energy Agency has significantly underestimated oil demand growth over the past decade, with 2025 actual demand already exceeding their 2030 forecast. This pattern suggests the IEA's energy demand projections may be unreliably low for market planning purposes.

posts.text: Over the past seven weeks, managed money's net long position in Brent crude has collapsed from a 7½-year high of 496k contracts to a 2026 low at 114k contracts in the reporting week ending 16 June, when prospects of a US-Iran peace deal gathered momentum and Brent subsequently https://t.co/IP1pJPCahF

posts.text:
DoC liquids supply market share to increase after non-DoC supply peaks in the 2030s.

By the 2030s, non-DoC supply is projected to peak and experience a long plateau at around 60 mb/d. Canada and Argentina stand out among non-DoC producers as countries where supply is forecast to https://t.co/jSGV7arPGa
posts.summary: OPEC projects that non-member oil production will peak in the 2030s at approximately 60 million barrels per day, after which OPEC's market share is expected to increase as non-OPEC supply plateaus. Canada and Argentina are identified as notable exceptions among non-OPEC producers with anticipated supply growth during this period.

posts.text: BREAKING: According to GasBuddy data, the U.S. average price of diesel has dropped to $4.99/gal, the first time it has fallen below $5 since March 15.

posts.text: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is now closed. The US says it remains open and that 17 million barrels of oil crossed the waterway today (that’s higher than pre-war, and a lot higher when the bypass pipelines volumes are added). https://t.co/LbkE3Iq9Ya
posts.summary: Iran claims the Strait of Hormuz is closed, but the US reports that 17 million barrels of oil transited the strait today, exceeding pre-war volumes and remaining higher when accounting for bypass pipeline capacity. The conflicting statements suggest oil flows through the critical chokepoint are continuing at elevated levels despite Iran's assertion.

posts.text:
America will never come remotely close to running out of hydrocarbons.
 
@SecretaryWright: "90% of the oil and gas and coal that were underground 500 years ago will be underground a million years from now."https://t.co/bsAVynwll1
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Implications Summary

The Department of Energy stated that the US has vast untapped hydrocarbon reserves, with Secretary Wright asserting that 90% of underground oil, gas, and coal deposits will remain unexploited even over a million-year timeframe. This characterization suggests abundant long-term domestic supply availability, though the statement does not address current extraction rates, economic viability, or market dynamics.

posts.text: Nationwide  gas average slips to $3.95 while diesel plummets to $5.062/gal. News of another Strait of Hormuz closure won't arrest further price drops this week, with plenty of "catching up" to previous  wholesale moves. But an on again/off again Strait looms for summer.
posts.summary: Gas prices are declining toward $3.95 per gallon while diesel has fallen sharply to $5.062, with further drops expected this week despite potential supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz. Uncertainty around intermittent Strait closures poses a risk to sustained price declines heading into summer.

posts.text:
Global energy spending is on track to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026

Around $2.2 trillion of this is set to go to grids, storage, low-emissions fuels, nuclear, renewables, efficiency & electrification

And about $1.2 trillion goes to oil, natural gas & coal: https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/ODvGsvloiQ
posts.summary: Global energy spending is projected to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026, with approximately $1.2 trillion allocated to fossil fuels including oil, natural gas, and coal. This represents a significant minority of total energy investment, with the majority ($2.2 trillion) directed toward clean energy infrastructure and electrification.

posts.text:
Global energy demand growth was met by diverse range of sources in 2025 – led by solar & then gas.

While growth slowed from the previous year against a complex economic and geopolitical backdrop, electricity use outpaced overall demand.

More 👉 https://t.co/c9Z4hPfenX https://t.co/q4qHxkQDGr
posts.summary: The IEA reported that 2025 global energy demand growth was primarily met by solar and gas expansion rather than oil, suggesting continued displacement of oil's role in the energy mix. With electricity demand outpacing overall energy growth, oil's traditional dominance in meeting incremental energy needs appears to be weakening.

From a sectoral perspective, oil demand growth out to 2050 is set to be driven by road

posts.text: Joint Maritime Information Center says oil tankers and other vessels can use the southern (Omani) shipping lane of the Strait of Hormuz with “AIS on, radars radiating, running lights on, and normal use of VHF” either at night or day. https://t.co/f7Yyv9SIs5
posts.summary: The Joint Maritime Information Center has cleared vessels to transit the southern Omani shipping lane of the Strait of Hormuz with standard navigation equipment and procedures active, suggesting continued safe passage for oil tankers through this critical chokepoint.

posts.text: From New York to California, clean energy targets are being softened amid political and legal pressure. But with solar now among the cheapest sources of new power, critics argue the answer isn't weaker climate goals—it's faster permitting and more deployment. #CleanEnergy
posts.summary: Clean energy targets across major U.S. states are facing pressure to weaken, though solar's competitive cost suggests the solution lies in accelerating permitting and deployment rather than scaling back climate goals. The shift could have implications for oil demand if clean energy expansion proceeds faster through regulatory streamlining rather than policy rollbacks.

posts.text: The race for Helium-3 is heating up. While lunar regolith could provide vast future supplies, Earth-based sources remain the only practical option today. Cost, infrastructure, and accessibility continue to give terrestrial production a decisive advantage. #Helium3 #SpaceMining
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Implications

Currently, Earth-based helium-3 production remains more economically viable than lunar extraction due to lower costs and established infrastructure, though lunar resources may become competitive in the future as space mining technology develops.

posts.text: Brent crude rebounded above $80/bbl after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to halt fighting in southern Lebanon, easing fears that renewed conflict could derail the fragile U.S.-Iran peace process and delay the return of disrupted Middle East oil supplies. #OilMarkets #MiddleEast
posts.summary: Brent crude rose above $80 per barrel following a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, as the reduced conflict risk supports hopes for resumed Middle East oil supplies and continued U.S.-Iran diplomatic progress.

posts.text: BP is weighing a sale of its UK upstream portfolio for up to $2.7 billion as it pivots toward higher-growth regions. The move would support its $20 billion divestment target while exploration successes in Brazil, Namibia, Egypt and Angola reshape its future. #BP #OilAndGas
posts.summary: BP is divesting its UK upstream assets for up to $2.7 billion to accelerate its $20 billion divestment target and reallocate capital toward higher-growth exploration regions in Brazil, Namibia, Egypt, and Angola. The shift signals a strategic retreat from mature North Sea production in favor of emerging offshore opportunities in Africa and South America.

posts.text:
U.S. oil and gas rig count edged up one rig to 563 for the week ending June 18 according to Baker Hughes data, with the Niobrara (+1) the week's sole basin mover as all other basins held steady.
https://t.co/vF0rNysWRy
posts.summary: U.S. oil and gas rig count increased modestly to 563 for the week ending June 18, driven solely by a one-rig addition in the Niobrara basin, suggesting minimal changes in drilling activity across major U.S. oil-producing regions.

posts.text: Russia is rationing gasoline in Moscow after Ukrainian drone strikes knocked out key refining capacity. The Moscow refinery, which supplies up to 40% of the capital’s fuel, was hit as outages removed an estimated 600,000 bpd of refining capacity. #Russia #Oil #Refining
posts.summary: Ukrainian drone strikes have damaged Russian refining capacity, with the Moscow refinery hit and an estimated 600,000 barrels per day of capacity removed, prompting fuel rationing in Moscow. The loss of a facility supplying 40% of the capital's fuel supply could create significant supply constraints in the Russian market.

posts.text: #Highlights: 20th Edition of the #OPEC World Oil Outlook (2026) launched via livestream on 18 June 2026 in Vienna, Austria. https://t.co/4gVQElPSYD
posts.summary: OPEC released the 20th edition of its World Oil Outlook on June 18, 2026, providing updated forecasts and analysis on global oil market trends. The outlook serves as a key reference document for understanding OPEC's perspective on long-term oil demand, supply, and market dynamics.

posts.text:
Our bal-summer Henry Hub forecast sits above the forward curve, but the bigger call is on the ceiling. Withlittle new LNG capacity due online before H2 2027, sustained prices above $3.50/MMBtu outside winter look unlikely.

Strong production growth and a comfortable 3.91 tcf https://t.co/vrUiyoo2XH
posts.summary: Energy Aspects forecasts natural gas prices will be capped below $3.50/MMBtu outside winter months due to limited new LNG export capacity coming online before late 2027, despite strong domestic production growth. While the analysis focuses on the gas market, sustained lower energy prices could have modest downward pressure on crude oil valuations.

posts.text: Juneteenth is a federal holiday marking June 19, 1865, when enslaved people in Texas were informed the Civil War had ended and they were free.
